control_name: Limit system access to authorized users
description: Restrict logical access to authorized users, processes, and devices.
notes: Repo provides password policy + Access Analyzer; client wires SSO and least-privilege roles. SSP-written.

description: Restrict authorized transactions and functions for authorized users.
Control approved authorizations for controlling CUI flow within the system.
notes: Tiered subnets + endpoint SGs; client adds workload SGs / NACLs.

description: Separate duties of individuals to reduce risk of malevolent activity.
Employ least privilege, including for specific security functions.
Use non-privileged accounts when accessing non-security functions.
notes: Operational practice; enforce via SSO permission sets.

control_name: Prevent non-privileged users from privileged functions
description: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions.
Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.
notes: Configured at the IdP (Okta/Entra/IAM Identity Center).

description: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with applicable CUI rules.
notes: Banner enforced at workstation / SSO portal layer.

description: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays.
Terminate user sessions after a defined condition.
notes: Session timeouts configured at IdP / app layer.

description: Monitor and control remote access sessions.
notes: SSM Session Manager + CloudTrail logs sessions.

description: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect remote access sessions.
notes: DenyNonFipsEndpoints + SSM TLS; client manages bastion certs.

control_name: Route remote access via managed access points
description: Route remote access via managed access control points.
notes: VPC endpoints + SSM Session Manager as the bastion.

control_name: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands
description: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and remote access to security-relevant info.
Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections.
Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption.
Control connection of mobile devices.
Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.
Verify and control connections to and use of external systems.
notes: VPC endpoints reduce external exposure; client-controlled egress lists.

description: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.
Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.
notes: Demo workload deliberately serves no CUI; client governs publishing.

description: Ensure managers, system administrators, and users are aware of security risks.
Ensure personnel are trained for assigned security duties.
Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting insider threats.
Create and retain system audit logs and records.
notes: Multi-region trail, KMS-encrypted, Object-Locked S3, CW Logs metric filters.

description: Ensure actions of individual users can be uniquely traced.
notes: userIdentity in every CloudTrail record; named SSO principals.

description: Review and update logged events.
notes: Repo provisions storage; client owns log review cadence.

description: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.
notes: Wire alarms on CloudTrail/Config delivery failures in workload root.

description: Correlate audit record review for investigation and response.
notes: SIEM integration is downstream of this repo.

description: Provide audit record reduction and report generation.
Provide a system capability to compare and synchronize internal clocks with an authoritative source.
notes: AWS provides NTP; client EC2 AMIs must use it.

control_name: Protect audit information from unauthorized modification
description: Protect audit information and tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.
notes: Object Lock GOVERNANCE + KMS SSE + bucket policy deny-non-TLS; client must restrict CloudTrail:Stop/Delete via SCP.

description: Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users.
notes: Use SCP to deny CloudTrail:Stop/Delete except for audit role.

description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories.
notes: Config recorder captures baselines; SSM Inventory for OS-level is client responsibility.

description: Establish and enforce security configuration settings.
notes: Optional NIST 800-171 conformance pack hook; client supplies YAML body. SSP-written.

description: Track, review, approve/disapprove, and audit changes.
notes: Repo captures change events; client owns CAB process.

description: Analyze security impact of changes prior to implementation.
Define, document, approve, enforce physical and logical access restrictions for changes.
Employ principle of least functionality (disable unnecessary services).
notes: Minimal VPC endpoints; client hardens AMIs.

description: Restrict, disable, or prevent use of nonessential programs, functions, ports.
notes: Default SGs deny all; client must justify open ports.

description: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) or permit-by-exception (whitelisting) for software.
Control and monitor user-installed software.
Identify system users, processes, and devices.
notes: Named SSO users; client federates from IdP.

description: Authenticate the identities of users, processes, and devices.

control_name: MFA for privileged accounts and network access
description: Use multifactor authentication for privileged and network access for non-privileged accounts.
notes: Repo readies the foundation; MFA enforced at IdP. SSP-written.

description: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms.
notes: AWS APIs use SigV4; IdP must enforce phishing-resistant MFA.

description: Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period.
Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity.
Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created.
notes: Min length 14, all character classes; covered by SSP §3.5.3 write-up.

description: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

control_name: Allow temporary passwords for system logons
description: Allow temporary password use for system logons with immediate change to permanent.
Store and transmit only cryptographically protected passwords.
notes: AWS hashes IAM passwords; client uses Secrets Manager for app secrets.

description: Obscure feedback of authentication information.
Establish operational incident-handling capability.
Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials.
Test the organizational incident response capability.
Perform maintenance on organizational systems.
Provide effective controls on tools, techniques, and personnel for maintenance.
Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of CUI.
notes: AWS handles physical media; CUI in S3/EBS protected by KMS.

control_name: Check media for malicious code before maintenance
description: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code.
Require multifactor authentication to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions.

control_name: Supervise maintenance by personnel without authorization
description: Supervise maintenance activities of personnel without required access authorization.
Protect (physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI.
notes: AWS handles physical media; KMS encrypts logical media.

description: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users.
notes: KMS key policies + S3 bucket policies.

description: Sanitize or destroy system media containing CUI before disposal or release.
Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations.

control_name: Control access to media outside controlled areas
description: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability.

control_name: Cryptographic protection of CUI on transport media
description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect CUI on digital media during transport.
notes: FIPS-validated KMS; Snowball uses FIPS modules.

description: Control the use of removable media on system components.

control_name: Prohibit use of portable storage without identifiable owner
description: Prohibit use of portable storage devices when no identifiable owner.
Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations.
notes: KMS-encrypted backups; client wires AWS Backup vaults.

description: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to systems containing CUI.
Ensure CUI and systems are protected during and after personnel actions.
Limit physical access to organizational systems and equipment to authorized individuals.
notes: AWS data centers — see SOC reports.

description: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure.
Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.
Maintain audit logs of physical access.

control_name: Control and manage physical access devices
description: Control and manage physical access devices.

control_name: Enforce safeguarding measures at alternate work sites
description: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.
Periodically assess risk to organizational operations.
Scan for vulnerabilities periodically and when new vulnerabilities are identified.
Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.
Periodically assess the security controls.

control_name: Develop and implement plans of action
description: Develop and implement plans of action to correct deficiencies.
Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis.
Develop, document, and periodically update System Security Plans.
notes: Repo includes ssp/ skeleton; client maintains.

control_name: Monitor and control communications at boundaries
description: Monitor, control, and protect communications at external and key internal boundaries.
notes: Tiered subnets + endpoint SGs + flow logs; client adds AWS Network Firewall if needed. SSP-written.

control_name: Apply architecture and design principles for security
description: Employ architectural designs and engineering principles that promote effective security.

control_name: Separate user functionality from system management
description: Separate user functionality from system management functionality.
notes: Workload roles separate from admin roles.

control_name: Prevent unauthorized information transfer via shared resources
description: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.
notes: AWS Nitro provides hardware isolation; client picks tenancy.

control_name: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible components
description: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components separated from internal networks.
notes: Public/private/data subnet tiers; client owns workload placement. Covered by SSP §3.13.1.

description: Deny network communications by default and allow by exception.
notes: Default SGs deny all; client adds workload allows.

description: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously connecting and bypassing security.

control_name: Cryptographic protection of CUI in transit
description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI in transit.

control_name: Terminate network connections at end of session
description: Terminate network connections at end of session or after a defined period.
Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography in the system.
notes: CMKs with annual rotation; client must define rotation/destruction procedures. Covered by SSP §3.13.11.

description: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography to protect the confidentiality of CUI.
notes: GovCloud provider use_fips_endpoint=true; KMS uses FIPS 140-2 modules.

control_name: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices
description: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication.
Control and monitor the use of mobile code.
Control and monitor the use of VoIP technologies.
Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.
notes: SigV4 + TLS; app-layer mutual auth is workload concern.

control_name: Protect confidentiality of CUI at rest
description: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.
notes: KMS CMKs + SSE-KMS for repo-managed buckets; client must set on workload buckets. Covered by SSP §3.13.11.

description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner.
Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations.
notes: GuardDuty malware protection; endpoint EDR client-owned.

description: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action.
notes: Subscribe to AWS bulletins; route to ticketing.

description: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.
Perform periodic scans of the system and real-time scans of files from external sources.

control_name: Monitor for attacks and unauthorized use
description: Monitor systems including inbound/outbound communications for attacks.
notes: Three-source detection: GuardDuty + CloudTrail + Flow Logs.

description: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.
notes: Metric filters surface unusual access; client tunes alerts.
